0.q: ZxNHX (GenBank accession number: EU103624, encoding tonoplast Na+/H+ antiporter)… positive correlation between upregulation of ZxNHX and Na+ accumulation in leaves of Z. xanthoxylum treated with salt or drought, indicating that ZxNHX is involved in Na+ compartmentation in leaf.

the ZxNHX and ZxVP1-1 genes encoding tonoplast Na+/H+ antiporter and H+-PPase ... improved the osmoregulatory capacity in chimeric sugar beet through increased compartmentalization of ions into the vacuoles by enhancing the activity of proton pumps
The tonoplast Na+/H+ antiporter ZxNHX1 is essential for maintaining the salt-accumulation characteristics of Z. xanthoxylum by compartmentalizing Na+ into vacuoles
transgenic alfalfa lines expressing both tonoplast NXH and H+-PPase genes, ZxNHX and ZxVP1-1 from the xerophyte Zygophyllum xanthoxylum
the xerohalophyte Zygophyllum xanthoxylum efficiently compartmentalizes Na+ into vacuoles, mediated by ZxNHX.
we co-overexpressed two xerophyte genes, ZxNHX and ZxVP1-1 from Zygophyllum xanthoxylum, encoding vacuolar membrane NHX and H+-PPase, respectively, in alfalfa

1.q: co-expression of ZxNHX and ZxVP1-1 improved the osmoregulatory capacity in chimeric sugar beet through increased compartmentalization of ions into the vacuoles by enhancing the activity of proton pumps and thus mitigated Na+-toxicity for plants

Na+ compartmentalisation is mediated by a tonoplast Na+/H+ antiporter, which is driven through proton motive force across tonoplast generated by the tonoplast H+-pumps ... H+-pyrophosphatase (H+-PPase)
tonoplast Cation/H+ antiporters (NHXs) and H+-pyrophosphatase (H+-PPase) play important roles in...vacuolar compartmentation of Na+, intracellular ions and pH homeostasis
Cation accumulation contributed to maintaining intracellular ions homoeostasis and osmoregulation of plants… as a result of co-expression of ZxNHX and ZxVP1-1.

5.q: ZxNHX is essential for controlling Na+, K+ uptake, long-distance transport and their homeostasis at whole-plant level via feedback regulation of the expression of genes involved in Na+, K+ transport.
ZxNHX1 has a stronger Na+ but weaker K+ sequestration capacity compared to AtNHX1; ZxNHX1-L2 displayed a significantly higher Na+ fluorescence intensity in vacuoles compared to AtNHX1-L2.

co-expression of ZxNHX and ZxVP1-1 improved the osmoregulatory capacity in chimeric sugar beet through increased compartmentalization of ions into the vacuoles by enhancing the activity of proton pumps
Cation accumulation contributed to maintaining intracellular ions homoeostasis and osmoregulation of plants

7.q: the silencing of ZxNHX1 ... dramatically increased that of ZxHKT1;1 in roots of Z. xanthoxylum under osmotic stress
In roots of L7, transcripts of ZxHKT1;1 increased by 53 %, whereas in WT NaCl induced only an 80 % increase... transport ability of ZxHKT1;1 outweighed that of ZxSOS1.

8.q: the silencing of ZxNHX1 ... reduced the transcript abundance of ZxSOS1 ... in roots of Z. xanthoxylum under osmotic stress
In roots of L7, NaCl (50 mM) increased transcripts of ZxSOS1 by only 10%, whereas in WT it induced a 620% increase; silencing of ZxNHX reduced ZxSOS1 upregulation.

9.q: Silencing of ZxNHX also triggered a downregulation of ZxAKT1 and ZxSKOR in roots, resulting in a significant decrease in K+ accumulation in all the tissues.
the expression level of ZxAKT1 significantly decreased by 40 and 41% in roots of L2 and L7, respectively, compared with WT

10.q: Silencing of ZxNHX also triggered a downregulation of ZxAKT1 and ZxSKOR in roots, resulting in a significant decrease in K+ accumulation in all the tissues.

11.q: the expression levels of genes encoding several transporters/channels related to K+/Na+ homeostasis, including ZxSKOR, ZxNHX, ZxSOS1 and ZxHKT1;1, were reduced in various tissues of a ZxAKT1-silenced line.

15.q: ZxNHX1 has a stronger Na+ but weaker K+ sequestration capacity compared to AtNHX1; ZxNHX1-L2 displayed a significantly higher Na+ fluorescence intensity in vacuoles compared to AtNHX1-L2.

Na+ compartmentalisation is mediated by a tonoplast Na+/H+ antiporter, which is driven through proton motive force across tonoplast generated by the tonoplast H+-pumps, H+-ATPase and H+-pyrophosphatase (H+-PPase).
